commit.message:
Add contextual Tips & Tricks section for remaining daily protein goal (#7)

Users had no in-app guidance on how to close the gap between their
current intake and daily protein goal. This adds a dynamic tips section
that surfaces food suggestions calibrated to the remaining amount.

### Changes

- **`index.html`** — New `#tips-section` container placed below Today's
Log; hidden by default via `display:none`
- **`app.js`**
  - `getTips(remaining)` — returns 3 contextual tips across 4 tiers:
    | Remaining | Suggestions |
    |-----------|-------------|
    | ≥ 60g | Chicken breast 200g, tuna + shake combo, meal planning |
    | 30–59g | Whey shake, tuna 100g, Greek yogurt + cottage cheese |
    | 10–29g | Greek yogurt 150g, two eggs, cottage cheese 100g |
    | < 10g | Milk 200ml, one egg, cottage cheese 50g |
- `renderTips()` — shows/hides section based on remaining; hidden once
goal is met
- `render()` now calls `renderTips()` so tips update live on every entry
change
- **`style.css`** — `.tip-item` uses a green left-border accent
(`border-left: 3px solid var(--green-500)`) and reuses existing
surface/text-muted variables; consistent with the log item design

commit.message:
feat: index title + og:description for richer semantic embeddings

Previously only the article title was embedded. Now each document is
indexed as 'Title. Description' using the og:description meta tag,
which contains a meaningful summary of the article content.

This significantly improves semantic search recall — queries about
concepts mentioned only in the article body (not the title) now match
correctly. Existing indexes should be rebuilt with build-index.

> <issue_description>## Summary

> Two places where errors are silently discarded, hiding real problems.

> ## Finding 1: `DirectoryList` drops `os.ReadDir` errors
> 
> **File:** `server/site.go`, `DirectoryList()` method
> 
> The line `files, _ := os.ReadDir(s.PathToData)` discards the error. A
permissions problem on the data directory produces a silent empty
listing instead of surfacing the issue.
> 
> **Fix:** Check the error and return it (or log it at Error level and
return an empty list with the error).
> 
> ## Finding 2: `applyMigrationsForPage` swallows migration errors
> 
> **File:** `server/site.go`, `applyMigrationsForPage()` method
> 
> When migration fails, it logs a warning and returns `content, nil` —
the caller thinks everything succeeded. A persistent migration bug would
silently produce un-migrated content on every read.
> 
> **Fix:** Return the error to the caller. Let the caller decide whether
to use the original content or fail. At minimum, the function
signature's error return should be honest.
> 
> ## Acceptance criteria
> - [ ] `DirectoryList` handles `os.ReadDir` errors (log + return error,
or propagate)
> - [ ] `applyMigrationsForPage` returns the error instead of swallowing
it
> - [ ] Callers of `applyMigrationsForPage` handle the new error
appropriately
> - [ ] Existing tests still pass</issue_description>

* feat: C-3 fullsync zlib compression, H-1 edge UDP encryption, M-6 channel index

C-3: Hub compresses RPC responses >4096 bytes with zlib (0x01 prefix).
Edge decompresses in handle_incoming_slot before protobuf decode.
Fullsync timeout increased 30s→60s.

hmac_secret. New packet types 0x11/0x12 for encrypted direct/relay
voice. route_voice() pre-encrypts once per packet for all peers.
Plaintext fallback when hmac_secret is absent.

M-6: channel_to_sessions reverse index in ChannelManager.
upsert/remove/load methods maintain the index. get_remote_users_in_channels
now O(|channels|×|sessions_per_channel|) instead of O(N).

fix(editor): use clipPath bounds for selection instead of unclipped getBBox

getBBox() ignores SVG clipPath, returning the full unclipped path
geometry. For steep functions like tan(x), this produced selection
bounds 2-5x larger than the visible clipped area.

measureElement now detects clipPath > rect inside the element and
uses its dimensions as the authoritative bounds. Also fixes the
property-based fallback (originBasedBounds) to prefer yClamp over
the missing range property for FunctionPlot elements.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: reduce FunctionPlot soft limit from 5x to 3x yClamp range

The soft limit used range*2 on each side (5x total range) but was
intended to be 3x per the original commit. This caused the path
geometry to extend far beyond the visible clip area, making the
SVG element's bounding box much larger than the visual bounds.
